verses.latin_vulgate: Vitis frondosa Israël,  fructus adæquatus est ei :  secundum multitudinem fructus sui multiplicavit altaria,  juxta ubertatem terræ suæ exuberavit simulacris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גֶּ֤פֶן בּוֹקֵק֙ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל פְּרִ֖י יְשַׁוֶּה־לּ֑וֹ כְּרֹ֣ב לְפִרְי֗וֹ הִרְבָּה֙ לַֽמִּזְבְּח֔וֹת כְּט֣וֹב לְאַרְצ֔וֹ הֵיטִ֖יבוּ מַצֵּבֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: Israel is an empty vine, he bringeth forth fruit unto himself: according to the multitude of his fruit he hath increased the altars; according to the goodness of his land they have made goodly images.

verses.latin_vulgate: Divisum est cor eorum, nunc interibunt ;  ipse confringet simulacra eorum,  depopulabitur aras eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָלַ֥ק לִבָּ֖ם עַתָּ֣ה יֶאְשָׁ֑מוּ ה֚וּא יַעֲרֹ֣ף מִזְבְּחוֹתָ֔ם יְשֹׁדֵ֖ד מַצֵּבוֹתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Their heart is divided; now shall they be found faulty: he shall break down their altars, he shall spoil their images.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia nunc dicent : Non est rex nobis,  non enim timemus Dominum ;  et rex quid faciet nobis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י עַתָּה֙ יֹֽאמְר֔וּ אֵ֥ין מֶ֖לֶךְ לָ֑נוּ כִּ֣י לֹ֤א יָרֵ֙אנוּ֙ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֔ה וְהַמֶּ֖לֶךְ מַה־יַּעֲשֶׂה־לָּֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: For now they shall say, We have no king, because we feared not the Lord; what then should a king do to us?

verses.latin_vulgate: Loquimini verba visionis inutilis, et ferietis fœdus ;  et germinabit quasi amaritudo judicium super sulcos agri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דִּבְּר֣וּ דְבָרִ֔ים אָל֥וֹת שָׁ֖וְא כָּרֹ֣ת בְּרִ֑ית וּפָרַ֤ח כָּרֹאשׁ֙ מִשְׁפָּ֔ט עַ֖ל תַּלְמֵ֥י שָׂדָֽי׃
verses.king_james: They have spoken words, swearing falsely in making a covenant: thus judgment springeth up as hemlock in the furrows of the field.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vaccas Bethaven coluerunt habitatores Samariæ ;  quia luxit super eum populus ejus,  et æditui ejus super eum exsultaverunt  in gloria ejus, quia migravit ab eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְעֶגְלוֹת֙ בֵּ֣ית אָ֔וֶן יָג֖וּרוּ שְׁכַ֣ן שֹׁמְר֑וֹן כִּֽי־אָבַ֨ל עָלָ֜יו עַמּ֗וֹ וּכְמָרָיו֙ עָלָ֣יו יָגִ֔ילוּ עַל־כְּבוֹד֖וֹ כִּי־גָלָ֥ה מִמֶּֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: The inhabitants of Samaria shall fear because of the calves of Beth–aven: for the people thereof shall mourn over it, and the priests thereof that rejoiced on it, for the glory thereof, because it is departed from it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Siquidem et ipse in Assur delatus est,  munus regi ultori.  Confusio Ephraim capiet,  et confundetur Israël in voluntate sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־אוֹתוֹ֙ לְאַשּׁ֣וּר יוּבָ֔ל מִנְחָ֖ה לְמֶ֣לֶךְ יָרֵ֑ב בׇּשְׁנָה֙ אֶפְרַ֣יִם יִקָּ֔ח וְיֵב֥וֹשׁ יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל מֵעֲצָתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: It shall be also carried unto Assyria for a present to king Jareb: Ephraim shall receive shame, and Israel shall be ashamed of his own counsel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Transire fecit Samaria regem suum  quasi spumam super faciem aquæ.
verses.king_james: As for Samaria, her king is cut off as the foam upon the water.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et disperdentur excelsa idoli, peccatum Israël ;  lappa et tribulus ascendet super aras eorum :  et dicent montibus : Operite nos,  et collibus : Cadite super nos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנִשְׁמְד֞וּ בָּמ֣וֹת אָ֗וֶן חַטַּאת֙ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל ק֣וֹץ וְדַרְדַּ֔ר יַעֲלֶ֖ה עַל־מִזְבְּחוֹתָ֑ם וְאָמְר֤וּ לֶהָרִים֙ כַּסּ֔וּנוּ וְלַגְּבָע֖וֹת נִפְל֥וּ עָלֵֽינוּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: The high places also of Aven, the sin of Israel, shall be destroyed: the thorn and the thistle shall come up on their altars; and they shall say to the mountains, Cover us; and to the hills, Fall on us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ex diebus Gabaa peccavit Israël ;  ibi steterunt.  Non comprehendet eos in Gabaa  prælium super filios iniquitatis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִימֵי֙ הַגִּבְעָ֔ה חָטָ֖אתָ יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל שָׁ֣ם עָמָ֔דוּ לֹא־תַשִּׂיגֵ֧ם בַּגִּבְעָ֛ה מִלְחָמָ֖ה עַל־בְּנֵ֥י עַֽלְוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: O Israel, thou hast sinned from the days of Gibeah: there they stood: the battle in Gibeah against the children of iniquity did not overtake them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Juxta desiderium meum corripiam eos :  congregabuntur super eos populi,  cum corripientur propter duas iniquitates suas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּאַוָּתִ֖י וְאֶסֳּרֵ֑ם וְאֻסְּפ֤וּ עֲלֵיהֶם֙ עַמִּ֔ים בְּאׇסְרָ֖ם לִשְׁתֵּ֥י (עינתם) [עוֹנֹתָֽם]׃
verses.king_james: It is in my desire that I should chastise them; and the people shall be gathered against them, when they shall bind themselves in their two furrows.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ephraim vitula docta diligere trituram,  et ego transivi super pulchritudinem colli ejus :  ascendam super Ephraim,  arabit Judas ;  confringet sibi sulcos Jacob.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶפְרַ֜יִם עֶגְלָ֤ה מְלֻמָּדָה֙ אֹהַ֣בְתִּי לָד֔וּשׁ וַאֲנִ֣י עָבַ֔רְתִּי עַל־ט֖וּב צַוָּארָ֑הּ אַרְכִּ֤יב אֶפְרַ֙יִם֙ יַחֲר֣וֹשׁ יְהוּדָ֔ה יְשַׂדֶּד־ל֖וֹ יַעֲקֹֽב׃
verses.king_james: And Ephraim is as an heifer that is taught, and loveth to tread out the corn; but I passed over upon her fair neck: I will make Ephraim to ride; Judah shall plow, and Jacob shall break his clods.

verses.latin_vulgate: Seminate vobis in justitia,  et metite in ore misericordiæ.  Innovate vobis novale ;  tempus autem requirendi Dominum,  cum venerit qui docebit vos justitiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זִרְע֨וּ לָכֶ֤ם לִצְדָקָה֙ קִצְר֣וּ לְפִי־חֶ֔סֶד נִ֥ירוּ לָכֶ֖ם נִ֑יר וְעֵת֙ לִדְר֣וֹשׁ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֔ה עַד־יָב֕וֹא וְיֹרֶ֥ה צֶ֖דֶק לָכֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Sow to yourselves in righteousness, reap in mercy; break up your fallow ground: for it is time to seek the Lord, till he come and rain righteousness upon you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Arastis impietatem,  iniquitatem messuistis :  comedistis frugem mendacii,  quia confisus es in viis tuis,  in multitudine fortium tuorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֲרַשְׁתֶּם־רֶ֛שַׁע עַוְלָ֥תָה קְצַרְתֶּ֖ם אֲכַלְתֶּ֣ם פְּרִי־כָ֑חַשׁ כִּי־בָטַ֥חְתָּ בְדַרְכְּךָ֖ בְּרֹ֥ב גִּבּוֹרֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: Ye have plowed wickedness, ye have reaped iniquity; ye have eaten the fruit of lies: because thou didst trust in thy way, in the multitude of thy mighty men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Consurget tumultus in populo tuo ;  et omnes munitiones tuæ vastabuntur,  sicut vastatus est Salmana a domo ejus qui judicavit Baal  in die prælii, matre super filios allisa.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְקָ֣אם שָׁאוֹן֮ בְּעַמֶּ֒ךָ֒ וְכׇל־מִבְצָרֶ֣יךָ יוּשַּׁ֔ד כְּשֹׁ֧ד שַֽׁלְמַ֛ן בֵּ֥ית אַֽרְבֵ֖אל בְּי֣וֹם מִלְחָמָ֑ה אֵ֥ם עַל־בָּנִ֖ים רֻטָּֽשָׁה׃
verses.king_james: Therefore shall a tumult arise among thy people, and all thy fortresses shall be spoiled, as Shalman spoiled Beth–arbel in the day of battle: the mother was dashed in pieces upon her children.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic fecit vobis Bethel,  a facie malitiæ nequitiarum vestrarum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כָּ֗כָה עָשָׂ֤ה לָכֶם֙ בֵּֽית־אֵ֔ל מִפְּנֵ֖י רָעַ֣ת רָעַתְכֶ֑ם בַּשַּׁ֕חַר נִדְמֹ֥ה נִדְמָ֖ה מֶ֥לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: So shall Beth–el do unto you because of your great wickedness: in a morning shall the king of Israel utterly be cut off.
